heading: Art. 141. Status
label: 941 Art. 141. Status
text: There is a court of record known as the United States Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces. The court is established under article I of the Constitution. The court is located for administrative purposes only in the Department of Defense.
notes: Amendments1994— substituted “Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces” for “Court of Military Appeals”.Pub. L. 103–337

Change of Name, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title IX, § 924(a)(1)108 Stat. 2831Oct. 5, 1994“The United States Court of Military Appeals shall hereafter be known and designated as the United States Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces.”
